---
title: "Bayesian Modeling of STIR Effects on Water Quality"
author: "AJ Brown"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = TRUE,
  warning = TRUE
)

# Core libs
library(rethinking)
library(posterior)
library(cmdstanr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggridges)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)
```


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# If you rely on helper functions (e.g., make_year_dist_mat), source them here:
source("./stir-bayes-backend.R")
model_version <- if (exists("model_version")) model_version else "v1p8"
```

```{r helpers-nonneg-email, include=FALSE}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper utilities for physically admissible predictions and optional
# sampling-complete email notification.
#
# Rationale for non-negativity helper:
# v1p8 is intentionally left on the original Gaussian modeling scale to
# preserve the better sampler behavior we observed relative to v1p9.
# To avoid impossible reported predictions, we project concentration and
# volume predictions onto the feasible domain only at prediction time.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
clamp_nonnegative <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  x[is.finite(x)] <- pmax(x[is.finite(x)], 0)
  x
}

# Optional email notification after long sampling runs.
# To enable, define these environment variables before knitting/running:
#   STAN_NOTIFY_TO
#   STAN_NOTIFY_FROM
#   STAN_NOTIFY_USER
#   STAN_NOTIFY_PASS
#   STAN_NOTIFY_HOST   (for example: smtp.gmail.com)
#   STAN_NOTIFY_PORT   (for example: 465 or 587)
# Safe to leave in place when SMTP credentials are not set.
notify_sampling_done <- function(subject = NULL, body = NULL) {
  required <- c(
    "STAN_NOTIFY_TO",
    "STAN_NOTIFY_FROM",
    "STAN_NOTIFY_USER",
    "STAN_NOTIFY_PASS",
    "STAN_NOTIFY_HOST",
    "STAN_NOTIFY_PORT"
  )

  vals <- Sys.getenv(required, unset = "")
  names(vals) <- required

  if (any(vals == "")) {
    missing_vars <- names(vals)[vals == ""]
    message(
      "Sampling-finished email skipped: missing environment variable(s): ",
      paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", ")
    )
    return(invisible(FALSE))
  }

  if (!requireNamespace("blastula", quietly = TRUE)) {
    message("Sampling-finished email skipped: install.packages('blastula') is required.")
    return(invisible(FALSE))
  }

  smtp_port <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(Sys.getenv("STAN_NOTIFY_PORT")))
  if (is.na(smtp_port)) {
    message("Sampling-finished email skipped: STAN_NOTIFY_PORT is not a valid integer.")
    return(invisible(FALSE))
  }

  use_ssl <- identical(smtp_port, 465L)

  if (is.null(subject)) {
    subject <- sprintf("Stan sampling finished: %s", model_version)
  }

  if (is.null(body)) {
    body <- paste0(
      "Your CmdStan fit finished sampling.

",
      "Model version: ", model_version, "
",
      "Time finished: ", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
    )
  }

  email_obj <- blastula::compose_email(
    body = blastula::md(body)
  )

  tryCatch({
    blastula::smtp_send(
      email = email_obj,
      from = Sys.getenv("STAN_NOTIFY_FROM"),
      to = Sys.getenv("STAN_NOTIFY_TO"),
      subject = subject,
      credentials = blastula::creds_envvar(
        user = Sys.getenv("STAN_NOTIFY_USER"),
        pass_envvar = "STAN_NOTIFY_PASS",
        host = Sys.getenv("STAN_NOTIFY_HOST"),
        port = smtp_port,
        use_ssl = use_ssl
      )
    )

    message("Sampling-finished email sent.")
    invisible(TRUE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Sampling-finished email failed: ", conditionMessage(e))
    invisible(FALSE)
  })
}
```

# Model 1.8 

* Joint Bayesian model for **OUT concentration and OUT volume** fit simultaneously
* Model uses multivariate normal (MVN) hierarchical structure for analyte-specific parameters
* Model is parametrized in **non-centered** form for better sampling efficiency
* **Analyte-specific** intercepts, STIR slopes, and inflow-concentration slopes
* **Volume** modeled as a function of STIR, with Bayesian imputation for missing VOL
* **Inflow concentration (CIN)** now handled via Bayesian imputation (`CIN ~ dnorm(0,1)`), not zero-substitution
* Includes all DAG-identified adjustment variables affecting concentration
  * { Analyte, Block, Inflow Conc., Irrigation Count, Outflow vol, Sampler Method, duplicate status, flume type }
* Full uncertainty propagation from CIN, VOL, STIR into analyte-specific concentration predictions
* Supports **scenario-based simulation** for STIR → Load via generative functions (conditional or averaged scenarios)
* Incorporates multi-output Gaussian Process (GP) on Year to capture temporal correlation in residuals for each analyte
* v1.5 only has cmdstan model, no rethinking/ulam version
* v1.6 adds crop type effects and residue cover at planting effects
* v1.7 promotes residue, volume, and inflow concentration to latent processes with full Bayesian imputation and joint propagation. Also uses new python wq_cleaned.csv, which has been tested to be identical to previous versions.


# 1. Load and lightly type-enforce the Bayes-clean dataset (from Python pipeline)

```{r load-clean}
# Prefer the Python-produced Bayes-clean dataset
clean_path <- "out/wq_cleaned.csv"
if (!file.exists(clean_path)) {
  alt <- file.path("..", clean_path)
  if (file.exists(alt)) {
    clean_path <- alt
  } else {
    stop(
      "Bayes-clean file not found. Expected 'out/wq_cleaned.csv'.
",
      "Run the Python pipeline first:
",
      "  python code/run_pipeline.py
"
    )
  }
}

wq_clean <- readr::read_csv(clean_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

abbr_levels <- c("NH4","ICP","NO3","NOx","NO2","NPOC","OP","Se","TDS","TKN","TN","TP","TSP","TSS")

# Defensive type enforcement (lightweight, no re-cleaning)
wq_clean <- wq_clean %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Dates
    Date        = if ("Date" %in% names(.)) as.Date(Date) else Date,
    PlantDate   = if ("PlantDate" %in% names(.)) as.Date(PlantDate) else PlantDate,
    HarvestDate = if ("HarvestDate" %in% names(.)) as.Date(HarvestDate) else HarvestDate,

    # Numeric/integer fields commonly used downstream
    Year        = if ("Year" %in% names(.)) as.integer(Year) else Year,
    SeasonYear  = if ("SeasonYear" %in% names(.)) as.integer(SeasonYear) else SeasonYear,

    # Factors/categoricals often used downstream
    Treatment    = if ("Treatment" %in% names(.)) factor(Treatment, levels = c("CT","MT","ST")) else Treatment,
    Rep          = if ("Rep" %in% names(.)) factor(Rep) else Rep,
    Irrigation   = if ("Irrigation" %in% names(.)) factor(Irrigation) else Irrigation,
    SampleMethod = if ("SampleMethod" %in% names(.)) factor(SampleMethod) else SampleMethod,
    FlumeMethod  = if ("FlumeMethod" %in% names(.)) factor(FlumeMethod) else FlumeMethod,
    Duplicate    = if ("Duplicate" %in% names(.)) as.integer(Duplicate) else Duplicate,
    Analyte      = if ("Analyte" %in% names(.)) factor(Analyte) else Analyte,
    analyte_abbr = if ("analyte_abbr" %in% names(.)) factor(analyte_abbr, levels = abbr_levels) else analyte_abbr,
    Crop         = if ("Crop" %in% names(.)) factor(Crop) else Crop,
    previous_crop = if ("previous_crop" %in% names(.)) factor(previous_crop) else NA,

    # Add original row number for tracking
    orig_row = dplyr::row_number()
  )


# If previous_crop was not provided by the pipeline, derive it as last year's Crop
# within plot (Treatment × Rep), and for the first observed year, set previous_crop = Crop.
if (!("previous_crop" %in% names(wq_clean)) || all(is.na(wq_clean$previous_crop))) {
  wq_clean <- wq_clean %>%
    group_by(Treatment, Rep) %>%
    arrange(Year, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(previous_crop = dplyr::lag(Crop)) %>%
    mutate(previous_crop = ifelse(is.na(previous_crop), as.character(Crop), as.character(previous_crop))) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(previous_crop = factor(previous_crop, levels = levels(factor(Crop))))
}

# Sanity checks (quick)
req_cols <- c("cout_z","cin_z","stir_season_z","volume_z","Treatment","Rep","SampleMethod","MeasureMethod","FlumeMethod","Irrigation","Duplicate","Crop","previous_crop","analyte_abbr","Result_mg_L","RL_mg_L")
missing_cols <- setdiff(req_cols, names(wq_clean))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  warning("Missing expected columns in wq_cleaned.csv: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

cat("Loaded Bayes-clean dataset:", clean_path, "
")
cat("Rows:", nrow(wq_clean), " | Columns:", ncol(wq_clean), "
")

dplyr::glimpse(wq_clean)
```

---

# 2. Prepare model-ready dataset

Select analytes for modeling:

```{r subset-analytes}
# analytes_keep <- c(
#   "OP", "TP", "ICP", "NO3", "TN", "TSS",
#   "TKN", "TSP", "NH4", "NPOC", "Se", "NO2",
#   "NOx", "TDS"
# )

analytes_keep <- c(
  "OP", "TP", "NO3", "TN", "TSS",
  "TKN", "NH4", "Se", "NO2", "TDS"
)

d_mod <- wq_clean %>%
  dplyr::filter(analyte_abbr %in% analytes_keep) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(mod_row = dplyr::row_number()) %>%
  droplevels()

na_table <- d_mod %>%
  dplyr::group_by(analyte_abbr) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    n_total   = dplyr::n(),
    n_value   = sum(!is.na(Result_mg_L)),
    n_na      = sum(is.na(Result_mg_L)),
    prop_na   = n_na / n_total, #proportion missing
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

na_table

```

Create analyte index and build data list for ulam:

```{r make-stan-dat}
# 2. Build indices and standardized predictors -----------------------------
# DROP-IN replacement (v1p8, corrected):
# - Ensures censored rows are NOT treated as missing in C by constructing C_vec (dummy-filled on censored rows)
# - Removes PrevCr_n from stan_dat (Stan uses Cr_n for both Cr and PrevCr indexing)
# - Adds explicit guardrails that catch the main failure modes before you run Stan

residue_metric <- "cover"  # "cover" or "drymass"

# ---- Required pipeline columns / fallbacks ----
vin_candidates <- c("vin_z","inflow_volume_z","inflow_vol_z","vin_std")
vin_col <- vin_candidates[vin_candidates %in% names(d_mod)][1]
if (is.na(vin_col)) {
  stop("No inflow-volume z-score column found. Expected one of: ", paste(vin_candidates, collapse = ", "))
}

irr_z_candidates <- c("irr_z","IRR_z","irr_num_z")
irr_z_col <- irr_z_candidates[irr_z_candidates %in% names(d_mod)][1]
if (is.na(irr_z_col)) {
  stop("No irrigation z-score column found. Expected one of: ", paste(irr_z_candidates, collapse = ", "),
       ". (Pipeline should provide IRR_z in v1p8 workflow.)")
}

# Censoring columns (pipeline-provided)
if (!("Result_is_nd" %in% names(d_mod))) {
  stop("Missing 'Result_is_nd' column (0/1). v1p8 expects pipeline to provide this.")
}
lod_candidates <- c("Result_lod_mg_L","RL_mg_L")
lod_col <- lod_candidates[lod_candidates %in% names(d_mod)][1]
if (is.na(lod_col)) {
  stop("Missing censoring limit column. Expected one of: ", paste(lod_candidates, collapse = ", "))
}

# ---- Mutate indices + covariates ----
d_mod <- d_mod %>%
  mutate(
    # analyte index
    A = as.integer(analyte_abbr),

    # Block (Rep) index
    B = as.integer(Rep),

    # Sampler method index
    S = as.integer(SampleMethod),

    # Flume type index
    Fu = as.integer(FlumeMethod),

    # Irrigation count as numeric (still used for filtering out storms; model uses IRR_z)
    irr_num = dplyr::case_when(
      as.character(Irrigation) %in% c("S1", "S2") ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ as.integer(readr::parse_number(as.character(Irrigation)))
    ),

    # Inflow concentration (standardized), keep NAs for Bayesian imputation
    cin_std = cin_z,

    # Inflow volume (standardized), keep NAs for Bayesian imputation
    vin_std = .data[[vin_col]],

    # Irrigation z-score (standardized), from pipeline
    irr_z = .data[[irr_z_col]],

    # Duplicate status as 0/1
    dup = as.integer(Duplicate),

    # Year index
    Y = as.integer(as.factor(Year)),

    # Crop type index (categorical)
    Cr = as.integer(Crop),

    # Previous crop type index (categorical, must align to same levels as Crop)
    PrevCr = as.integer(previous_crop),

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Residue percent cover -> proportion in (0,1) with epsilon adjustment
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    residue_prop = {
      x <- as.numeric(.data$Residue_PercentCover)
      x <- ifelse(is.na(x), NA_real_, ifelse(x > 1, x / 100, x))
      eps <- 1e-4
      ifelse(is.na(x), NA_real_, pmin(pmax(x, eps), 1 - eps))
    },

    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Residue dry mass (kg/m^2) -> log transform (<=0 becomes NA)
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    drymass_kg_m2 = as.numeric(.data$Residue_DryMass_kg_m2),
    drymass_log = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(drymass_kg_m2) | drymass_kg_m2 <= 0,
      NA_real_,
      log(drymass_kg_m2)
    )
  )

# Drop storm rows (no ordinal irrigation meaning)
d_mod <- d_mod %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(irr_num))

# Z-score drymass_log using observed values only (keeps NA as NA)
drymass_mu <- mean(d_mod$drymass_log, na.rm = TRUE)
drymass_sd <- sd(d_mod$drymass_log, na.rm = TRUE)

d_mod <- d_mod %>%
  mutate(
    drymass_log_z = (drymass_log - drymass_mu) / drymass_sd,

    # Single residue input used by Stan
    RES_input = dplyr::case_when(
      residue_metric == "cover"   ~ residue_prop,
      residue_metric == "drymass" ~ drymass_log_z,
      TRUE ~ residue_prop
    )
  )

# ---- Censoring vectors for Stan (C on cout_z scale) ----
C_cens <- as.integer(d_mod$Result_is_nd == 1)
lod_mg_L <- as.numeric(d_mod[[lod_col]])

# Convert mg/L LOD -> cout_z scale by analyte-specific linear mapping:
#   cout_z ≈ alpha_a + beta_a * log(Result_mg_L)  (fit using non-censored observations)
C_cens_limit <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(d_mod))

for (a in sort(unique(d_mod$A))) {
  idx_a <- which(d_mod$A == a)

  fit_idx <- idx_a[
    !is.na(d_mod$cout_z[idx_a]) &
      !is.na(d_mod$Result_mg_L[idx_a]) &
      (d_mod$Result_is_nd[idx_a] == 0) &
      (d_mod$Result_mg_L[idx_a] > 0) &
      !is.na(lod_mg_L[idx_a]) & (lod_mg_L[idx_a] > 0)
  ]

  if (length(fit_idx) >= 20) {
    x <- log(d_mod$Result_mg_L[fit_idx])
    y <- d_mod$cout_z[fit_idx]
    co <- coef(lm(y ~ x))
    alpha <- unname(co[1]); beta <- unname(co[2])
    C_cens_limit[idx_a] <- alpha + beta * log(lod_mg_L[idx_a])
  }
}

# Fallback for analytes with too few points for a stable mapping
if (anyNA(C_cens_limit)) {
  fit_all <- which(
    !is.na(d_mod$cout_z) &
      !is.na(d_mod$Result_mg_L) &
      (d_mod$Result_is_nd == 0) &
      (d_mod$Result_mg_L > 0) &
      !is.na(lod_mg_L) & (lod_mg_L > 0)
  )
  if (length(fit_all) < 50) {
    stop("Not enough non-censored observations to infer cout_z <-> log(mg/L) mapping for censoring limits.")
  }
  co_all <- coef(lm(d_mod$cout_z[fit_all] ~ log(d_mod$Result_mg_L[fit_all])))
  miss <- which(is.na(C_cens_limit))
  C_cens_limit[miss] <- unname(co_all[1]) + unname(co_all[2]) * log(lod_mg_L[miss])
}

# Safety: Stan only uses limit when C_cens==1, but avoid NA/Inf anyway
C_cens_limit[!is.finite(C_cens_limit)] <- 0

# ---- Critical fix: construct C_vec so censored rows are NOT treated as missing ----
# If cout_z is NA on ND rows, we provide a dummy finite value (ignored by Stan when C_cens==1).
C_vec <- d_mod$cout_z
C_vec[C_cens == 1 & is.na(C_vec)] <- 0

# instantiate distance matrix for gaussian process on year
years_used <- sort(unique(d_mod$Year))
mat <- make_year_dist_mat(years_used)

# lookup code for later
analyte_lookup <- d_mod %>%
  distinct(A, analyte_abbr) %>%
  arrange(A)

block_lookup <- d_mod %>%
  distinct(B, Rep) %>%
  arrange(B)

sampler_lookup <- d_mod %>%
  distinct(S, SampleMethod) %>%
  arrange(S)

flume_lookup <- d_mod %>%
  distinct(Fu, FlumeMethod) %>%
  arrange(Fu)

irrigation_lookup <- d_mod %>%
  distinct(irr_num, Irrigation) %>%
  arrange(irr_num)

duplicate_lookup <- d_mod %>%
  distinct(dup, Duplicate) %>%
  arrange(dup)

crop_lookup <- d_mod %>%
  distinct(Cr, Crop) %>%
  arrange(Cr)

prevcrop_lookup <- d_mod %>%
  distinct(PrevCr, previous_crop) %>%
  arrange(PrevCr)

# prepare data list for stan
stan_dat <- list(
  N    = nrow(d_mod),

  # group counts
  A_n  = length(unique(d_mod$A)),
  B_n  = length(unique(d_mod$B)),
  S_n  = length(unique(d_mod$S)),
  F_n  = length(unique(d_mod$Fu)),
  D_n  = length(unique(d_mod$dup)),
  Y_n  = length(unique(d_mod$Y)),
  Cr_n = length(unique(d_mod$Cr)),

  # indices
  A    = d_mod$A,
  B    = d_mod$B,
  S    = d_mod$S,
  Fu   = d_mod$Fu,
  Y    = d_mod$Y,
  Cr   = d_mod$Cr,
  PrevCr = d_mod$PrevCr,

  # distance matrix for year effects
  D    = mat,

  # outcome + censoring (use C_vec, not raw cout_z)
  C    = C_vec,
  C_cens = C_cens,
  C_cens_limit = C_cens_limit,

  # main exposure
  STIR = d_mod$stir_season_z,

  # other covariates from DAG
  CIN   = d_mod$cin_std,
  VOL   = d_mod$volume_z,
  IRR_z = d_mod$irr_z,
  VIN   = d_mod$vin_std,
  DUP   = d_mod$dup,

  # residue covariate (cover or drymass, depending on residue_metric)
  RES  = d_mod$RES_input
)

cat("\nCensoring summary:\n")
cat("  Censored (ND) rows:", sum(stan_dat$C_cens == 1), "\n")
cat("  Censored rows with finite limit:", sum(stan_dat$C_cens == 1 & is.finite(stan_dat$C_cens_limit)), "\n")
cat("  Censored rows with finite C:", sum(stan_dat$C_cens == 1 & is.finite(stan_dat$C)), "\n")

# Optional: quick guardrails
if (identical(residue_metric, "cover")) {
  stopifnot(all(is.na(stan_dat$RES) | (stan_dat$RES > 0 & stan_dat$RES < 1)))
}

# Additional quick checks for censoring inputs
stopifnot(length(stan_dat$C_cens) == stan_dat$N)
stopifnot(length(stan_dat$C_cens_limit) == stan_dat$N)
stopifnot(all(stan_dat$C_cens %in% c(0L, 1L)))

# Extra guardrails that specifically protect censoring correctness
if (any(C_cens == 1 & is.na(d_mod$cout_z))) {
  message("Note: cout_z is NA for some censored rows; using dummy C=0 for those rows so censoring likelihood is applied.")
}
stopifnot(all(is.finite(stan_dat$C[C_cens == 1])))
stopifnot(all(is.finite(stan_dat$C_cens_limit[C_cens == 1])))
#stopifnot(all(stan_dat$C_cens_limit[stan_dat$C_cens == 1] != 0))

```

```{r check-na-vars}
# Identify the stan_dat entries that are vectors of length N (i.e., row-level variables)
vec_names <- names(stan_dat)[sapply(stan_dat, function(x) length(x) == stan_dat$N)]

# Loop through these and report NAs
for (nm in vec_names) {
  vals <- stan_dat[[nm]]
  na_idx <- which(is.na(vals))

  cat("\n==============================\n")
  cat("Variable:", nm, "\n")

  if (length(na_idx) == 0) {
    cat("No NA values detected.\n")
  } else {
    cat("NA count:", length(na_idx), "\n")
    cat("Rows with NA:", paste(head(na_idx, 25), collapse = ", "),
        if (length(na_idx) > 25) "..." else "", "\n\n")

    # Show the corresponding d_mod rows
    cat("Example offending rows from d_mod:\n")
    print(d_mod[na_idx[1:min(10, length(na_idx))], ])
  }
}

```


# 3. Hierarchical Bayesian model

```{r cmdstan model fit}
# ======================================================================
# Fit cmdstan model (v1p8) with censoring + missingness (CIN/VIN/VOL/RES)
# CLEAN drop-in replacement for your "Fit cmdstan model" block
#
# Key points:
#   1) ND rows are handled as LEFT-CENSORED (do NOT mark them missing).
#   2) Truly missing C rows (and not censored) are handled via missidx.
#   3) Stan receives NO NA values in any vector.
#   4) Censoring limits are transformed onto the same scale as cout_z
#      using an analyte-specific mapping: cout_z ≈ alpha_a + beta_a*log(mg/L).
# ======================================================================

# Force recompile to ensure v1p8 is what runs
mod <- cmdstan_model("m_stir_mogp_v1p8.stan", force_recompile = TRUE)

# Start from base stan_dat you built earlier
stan_dat_cmd <- stan_dat

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Pull row-level vectors (length N)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
N <- stan_dat_cmd$N

C_raw   <- d_mod$cout_z
CIN_vec <- d_mod$cin_std
VIN_vec <- d_mod$vin_std
VOL_vec <- d_mod$volume_z

Cr_vec    <- d_mod$Cr
PrevCr_vec <- d_mod$PrevCr

# Residue input, use the same one you passed in stan_dat
# (RES_input is preferred because it already reflects residue_metric choice)
if ("RES_input" %in% names(d_mod)) {
  RES_vec_raw <- d_mod$RES_input
} else if ("residue_prop" %in% names(d_mod)) {
  RES_vec_raw <- d_mod$residue_prop
} else if ("Residue_PercentCover" %in% names(d_mod)) {
  RES_vec_raw <- as.numeric(d_mod$Residue_PercentCover)
  RES_vec_raw <- ifelse(is.na(RES_vec_raw), NA_real_,
                        ifelse(RES_vec_raw > 1, RES_vec_raw / 100, RES_vec_raw))
} else {
  stop("No residue column found. Expected one of: RES_input, residue_prop, Residue_PercentCover.")
}

stopifnot(
  length(C_raw)       == N,
  length(CIN_vec)     == N,
  length(VIN_vec)     == N,
  length(VOL_vec)     == N,
  length(Cr_vec)      == N,
  length(PrevCr_vec)  == N,
  length(RES_vec_raw) == N
)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Censoring: ND flag + RL/LOD, convert RL to cout_z scale
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
if (!("Result_is_nd" %in% names(d_mod))) {
  stop("Expected pipeline column 'Result_is_nd' (0/1) for v1p8 censoring.")
}
C_cens <- as.integer(d_mod$Result_is_nd == 1)

lod_candidates <- c("Result_lod_mg_L", "RL_mg_L")
lod_col <- lod_candidates[lod_candidates %in% names(d_mod)][1]
if (is.na(lod_col)) stop("Need 'Result_lod_mg_L' or 'RL_mg_L' to build censor limits.")
lod_mg_L <- as.numeric(d_mod[[lod_col]])

# Convert mg/L RL -> cout_z using analyte-specific mapping:
#   cout_z ≈ alpha_a + beta_a * log(Result_mg_L)
C_cens_limit <- rep(NA_real_, N)

for (a in sort(unique(d_mod$A))) {
  idx_a <- which(d_mod$A == a)

  fit_idx <- idx_a[
    (C_cens[idx_a] == 0L) &
      !is.na(C_raw[idx_a]) &
      !is.na(d_mod$Result_mg_L[idx_a]) & (d_mod$Result_mg_L[idx_a] > 0) &
      !is.na(lod_mg_L[idx_a]) & (lod_mg_L[idx_a] > 0)
  ]

  if (length(fit_idx) >= 20) {
    x <- log(d_mod$Result_mg_L[fit_idx])
    y <- C_raw[fit_idx]
    co <- coef(lm(y ~ x))
    alpha <- unname(co[1]); beta <- unname(co[2])
    C_cens_limit[idx_a] <- alpha + beta * log(lod_mg_L[idx_a])
  }
}

# Global fallback for any remaining limits
if (anyNA(C_cens_limit)) {
  fit_all <- which(
    (C_cens == 0L) &
      !is.na(C_raw) &
      !is.na(d_mod$Result_mg_L) & (d_mod$Result_mg_L > 0) &
      !is.na(lod_mg_L) & (lod_mg_L > 0)
  )
  if (length(fit_all) < 50) stop("Too few detected observations to infer censoring limit transform.")
  co_all <- coef(lm(C_raw[fit_all] ~ log(d_mod$Result_mg_L[fit_all])))

  miss <- which(is.na(C_cens_limit))
  C_cens_limit[miss] <- unname(co_all[1]) + unname(co_all[2]) * log(lod_mg_L[miss])
}

# Safety: censor limits must be finite for censored rows; non-censored rows can be anything finite
C_cens_limit[!is.finite(C_cens_limit)] <- 0

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Missingness for C: DO NOT drop censored rows
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Missing if C is NA AND NOT censored
is_C_miss <- as.integer(is.na(C_raw) & (C_cens == 0L))
C_missidx <- which(is_C_miss == 1L)

# Stan-safe C:
# - observed values stay
# - censored rows with NA get dummy finite value (ignored by Stan because C_cens==1)
# - true-missing rows filled with placeholder
C_clean <- as.numeric(C_raw)
C_clean[is.na(C_clean) & (C_cens == 1L)] <- 0
C_clean[is.na(C_clean) & (C_cens == 0L)] <- 0

# Guardrail: no censored row should be marked missing
stopifnot(all(is_C_miss[C_cens == 1L] == 0L))
stopifnot(all(is.finite(C_clean[C_cens == 1L])))
stopifnot(all(is.finite(C_cens_limit[C_cens == 1L])))

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Helpers for other missings (CIN/VIN/VOL/RES)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
prep_miss_gaussian <- function(x, fill = 0) {
  missidx <- which(is.na(x))
  x_clean <- as.numeric(x)
  if (length(missidx) > 0) x_clean[missidx] <- fill
  list(missidx = missidx, N_miss = length(missidx), clean = x_clean)
}

prep_miss_residue <- function(x, eps = 1e-4, fill = 0.5) {
  x_num <- as.numeric(x)
  missidx <- which(is.na(x_num))
  obs_idx <- setdiff(seq_along(x_num), missidx)

  if (length(obs_idx) > 0) {
    bad_nonfinite <- obs_idx[!is.finite(x_num[obs_idx])]
    if (length(bad_nonfinite) > 0) {
      stop("RES contains non-finite values at rows: ",
           paste(head(bad_nonfinite, 20), collapse = ", "),
           if (length(bad_nonfinite) > 20) " ..." else "")
    }
  }

  # If user accidentally passed 0–100 here, convert to 0–1
  if (length(obs_idx) > 0 && any(x_num[obs_idx] > 1, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    x_num[obs_idx] <- x_num[obs_idx] / 100
  }

  if (length(obs_idx) > 0) {
    bad_range <- obs_idx[x_num[obs_idx] < 0 | x_num[obs_idx] > 1]
    if (length(bad_range) > 0) {
      stop("RES has values outside [0,1] at rows: ",
           paste(head(bad_range, 20), collapse = ", "),
           if (length(bad_range) > 20) " ..." else "",
           "\nCheck residue scaling upstream (should be a 0–1 proportion).")
    }
  }

  if (length(obs_idx) > 0) {
    x_num[obs_idx] <- pmin(pmax(x_num[obs_idx], eps), 1 - eps)
  }

  if (length(missidx) > 0) x_num[missidx] <- fill

  list(missidx = missidx, N_miss = length(missidx), clean = x_num)
}

imp_CIN <- prep_miss_gaussian(CIN_vec, fill = 0)
imp_VIN <- prep_miss_gaussian(VIN_vec, fill = 0)
imp_VOL <- prep_miss_gaussian(VOL_vec, fill = 0)
imp_RES <- prep_miss_residue(RES_vec_raw, eps = 1e-4, fill = 0.5)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Populate cmdstan data list (NO NA allowed)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Concentration
stan_dat_cmd$C            <- C_clean
stan_dat_cmd$C_cens       <- as.array(as.integer(C_cens))
stan_dat_cmd$C_cens_limit <- as.numeric(C_cens_limit)

stan_dat_cmd$N_C_miss     <- length(C_missidx)
stan_dat_cmd$C_missidx    <- if (length(C_missidx) == 0) integer(0) else as.array(as.integer(C_missidx))
stan_dat_cmd$is_C_miss    <- as.array(as.integer(is_C_miss))

# CIN
stan_dat_cmd$CIN         <- imp_CIN$clean
stan_dat_cmd$N_CIN_miss  <- imp_CIN$N_miss
stan_dat_cmd$CIN_missidx <- if (imp_CIN$N_miss == 0) integer(0) else as.array(as.integer(imp_CIN$missidx))

# VIN
stan_dat_cmd$VIN         <- imp_VIN$clean
stan_dat_cmd$N_VIN_miss  <- imp_VIN$N_miss
stan_dat_cmd$VIN_missidx <- if (imp_VIN$N_miss == 0) integer(0) else as.array(as.integer(imp_VIN$missidx))

# VOL
stan_dat_cmd$VOL         <- imp_VOL$clean
stan_dat_cmd$N_VOL_miss  <- imp_VOL$N_miss
stan_dat_cmd$VOL_missidx <- if (imp_VOL$N_miss == 0) integer(0) else as.array(as.integer(imp_VOL$missidx))
stan_dat_cmd$is_VOL_miss <- as.array(as.integer(is.na(VOL_vec)))

# RES
stan_dat_cmd$RES         <- imp_RES$clean
stan_dat_cmd$N_RES_miss  <- imp_RES$N_miss
stan_dat_cmd$RES_missidx <- if (imp_RES$N_miss == 0) integer(0) else as.array(as.integer(imp_RES$missidx))
stan_dat_cmd$is_RES_miss <- as.array(as.integer(is.na(RES_vec_raw)))

# Crop indices (already integer-coded upstream)
Cr_n_now <- length(unique(Cr_vec))
stan_dat_cmd$Cr <- as.array(as.integer(Cr_vec))
if (is.null(stan_dat_cmd$Cr_n) || stan_dat_cmd$Cr_n != Cr_n_now) {
  stan_dat_cmd$Cr_n <- Cr_n_now
}

# Previous crop index (must be within 1..Cr_n as declared in Stan)
stan_dat_cmd$PrevCr <- as.array(as.integer(PrevCr_vec))

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Final consistency checks + helpful diagnostics
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(
  all(is.finite(stan_dat_cmd$C)),
  all(is.finite(stan_dat_cmd$CIN)),
  all(is.finite(stan_dat_cmd$VIN)),
  all(is.finite(stan_dat_cmd$VOL)),
  all(is.finite(stan_dat_cmd$RES)),
  all(stan_dat_cmd$RES > 0),
  all(stan_dat_cmd$RES < 1),
  all(stan_dat_cmd$C_cens %in% c(0L, 1L)),
  length(stan_dat_cmd$C_cens) == N,
  length(stan_dat_cmd$C_cens_limit) == N,
  isTRUE(all(stan_dat_cmd$Cr >= 1L)),
  isTRUE(all(stan_dat_cmd$Cr <= stan_dat_cmd$Cr_n)),
  isTRUE(all(stan_dat_cmd$PrevCr >= 1L)),
  isTRUE(all(stan_dat_cmd$PrevCr <= stan_dat_cmd$Cr_n))
)

cat("\n[INFO] Missing counts:\n")
cat("  N_C_miss    =", stan_dat_cmd$N_C_miss, "\n")
cat("  N_CIN_miss  =", stan_dat_cmd$N_CIN_miss, "\n")
cat("  N_VIN_miss  =", stan_dat_cmd$N_VIN_miss, "\n")
cat("  N_VOL_miss  =", stan_dat_cmd$N_VOL_miss, "\n")
cat("  N_RES_miss  =", stan_dat_cmd$N_RES_miss, "\n")
cat("[INFO] Crop levels (Cr_n) =", stan_dat_cmd$Cr_n, "\n")

cat("\n[INFO] Censoring summary:\n")
cat("  Censored (ND) rows:", sum(stan_dat_cmd$C_cens == 1L), "\n")
cat("  Censored rows with finite limit:",
    sum(stan_dat_cmd$C_cens == 1L & is.finite(stan_dat_cmd$C_cens_limit)), "\n")
cat("  Censored rows marked missing (should be 0):",
    sum(stan_dat_cmd$C_cens == 1L & stan_dat_cmd$is_C_miss == 1L), "\n")

cat("\n[INFO] Residue summary (raw, before clamp/fill):\n")
print(summary(RES_vec_raw))
cat("[INFO] Residue summary (Stan-ready, after clamp/fill):\n")
print(summary(stan_dat_cmd$RES))

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Sample with cmdstanr
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
dir.create("out_cmdstanr", showWarnings = FALSE)

fit <- mod$sample(
  data            = stan_dat_cmd,
  chains          = 4,
  parallel_chains = 4,
  iter_warmup     = 2000,
  iter_sampling   = 1000,
  refresh         = 100,
  show_messages   = TRUE,
  output_dir      = "out_cmdstanr",
  # save_warmup     = TRUE,
  adapt_delta     = 0.95,
  init            = 0.1,
  max_treedepth   = 15
)

```


```{r cmdstan model fit}
fit_rds_path <- "./out_cmdstanr/fit_mogp_v1p8.rds"
saveRDS(fit, file = fit_rds_path)
# fit <- readRDS(fit_rds_path)

# Optional email notification after fit serialization.
# This is intentionally non-fatal so overnight runs are not interrupted if
# SMTP credentials are missing or Gmail rejects the message.
notify_sampling_done(
  subject = sprintf("Stan sampling finished: %s", model_version),
  body = paste0(
    "Your CmdStan fit finished sampling.

",
    "Model version: ", model_version, "
",
    "Saved fit: ", normalizePath(fit_rds_path, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE), "
",
    "Time finished: ", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
  )
)

```


---

# 4. Posterior inspection

```{r}
# ============================================================
# v1p8 DIAGNOSTICS: key (non-latent) research parameters
# - Produces a clean table of R-hat + ESS (bulk/tail)
# - Uses robust regex-based inclusion/exclusion so it won't
#   break when parameter names change (e.g., gamma_CR removed)
#
# Assumes you have a cmdstanr fit object named: fit
# ============================================================

# --- Helper: get all variable names present in the fit ---
.all_vars <- function(fit) {
  # posterior::variables() works on draws objects
  draws <- fit$draws()
  posterior::variables(draws)
}

# --- Helper: filter to "important" parameters ---
pick_key_vars_v1p8 <- function(fit) {
  vars <- .all_vars(fit)

  # 1) Explicit INCLUDE patterns (edit freely)
  #    These are meant to capture your research-facing parameters:
  include_patterns <- c(
    # Global / analyte-level regression coefficients (common naming styles)
    "^alpha", "^beta", "^b_", "^a_",

    # GP hyperparameters (often key to year structure and mixing)
    "etasq", "rhosq", "rho", "eta", "sigma_year", "l_year", "ls_year",

    # Correlation / covariance structures (if you have multi-output components)
    "Omega", "L_Omega", "Sigma", "L_Sigma", "cholesky", "corr",

    # Observation / process SDs that often drive funnels & divergences
    "^sigma", "tau"
  )

  # 2) Explicit EXCLUDE patterns (these remove latent / per-row stuff)
  #    This is where you keep the list aggressive so you only see “research” params.
  exclude_patterns <- c(
    # latent truths & per-observation latent states
    "C_true", "V_true", "VOL_rep", "CIN_merge", "VIN_merge",
    "mu_C", "mu_V", "mu_res", "RES_star",

    # raw latent z vectors / imputation internals
    "^z_", "zC", "zV", "z_C", "z_V", "C_miss", "CIN_miss", "VIN_miss", "VOL_miss", "RES_miss",

    # posterior predictive per-row
    "_rep$", "rep\\[", "y_rep", "C_rep", "V_rep", "VOL_rep",

    # per-row log-lik arrays (if present)
    "log_lik", "lp__"
  )

  inc_re <- paste(include_patterns, collapse = "|")
  exc_re <- paste(exclude_patterns, collapse = "|")

  key_vars <- vars %>%
    # include anything that matches include patterns
    .[str_detect(., inc_re)] %>%
    # exclude latent / row-level / loglik etc
    .[!str_detect(., exc_re)] %>%
    # drop transformed parameters that are huge arrays unless you want them
    unique()

  # If you want to *force include* some known v1p8 names, add them here:
  # (These are optional; only kept if they actually exist in the fit.)
  force_include <- c(
    # Typical names in your lineage; safe because we intersect with vars
    "beta_stir", "beta_STIR", "b_stir",
    "beta_cin",  "beta_CIN",  "b_cin",
    "beta_vin",  "beta_VIN",  "b_vin",
    "beta_irr",  "beta_IRR",  "b_irr",
    "beta_dup",  "beta_DUP",  "b_dup",
    "beta_res",  "beta_RES",  "b_res",
    "beta_prevcr", "beta_PrevCr", "b_prevcr",
    "etasq_year", "rhosq_year"
  )
  key_vars <- unique(c(key_vars, intersect(force_include, vars)))

  key_vars
}

# --- Main: summarise key vars (Rhat + ESS) ---
summarise_key_vars <- function(fit, max_print = 250) {
  key_vars <- pick_key_vars_v1p8(fit)

  if (length(key_vars) == 0) {
    stop("No key variables matched. Consider relaxing include/exclude patterns in pick_key_vars_v1p8().")
  }

  summ <- fit$summary(variables = key_vars) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    # cmdstanr returns columns: variable, mean, median, sd, mad, q5, q95, rhat, ess_bulk, ess_tail
    arrange(desc(rhat), ess_bulk) %>%
    mutate(
      flag_rhat = if_else(is.finite(rhat) & rhat > 1.01, "CHECK", ""),
      flag_ess  = if_else(is.finite(ess_bulk) & ess_bulk < 200, "LOW_ESS", "")
    )

  cat("\n============================================================\n")
  cat("[v1p8] Key parameter diagnostics (sorted by worst R-hat)\n")
  cat("  Total key vars:", nrow(summ), "\n")
  cat("  R-hat > 1.01:", sum(summ$rhat > 1.01, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("  ESS bulk < 200:", sum(summ$ess_bulk < 200, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("============================================================\n\n")

  print(
    summ %>%
      select(variable, mean, sd, q5, q95, rhat, ess_bulk, ess_tail, flag_rhat, flag_ess) %>%
      head(max_print),
    n = min(max_print, nrow(summ))
  )

  invisible(summ)
}

# --- Optional: per-chain quick check for the worst offenders ---
trace_worst <- function(fit, summ_tbl, top_n = 12) {
  library(bayesplot)
  worst <- summ_tbl %>%
    filter(is.finite(rhat)) %>%
    arrange(desc(rhat), ess_bulk) %>%
    slice_head(n = top_n) %>%
    pull(variable)

  draws_array <- as_draws_array(fit$draws(variables = worst))

  # Trace + rank plots are the fastest way to see “one chain stuck”
  bayesplot::mcmc_trace(draws_array, pars = worst)
  bayesplot::mcmc_rank_overlay(draws_array, pars = worst)
}

# ============================================================
# RUN
# ============================================================
key_summary <- summarise_key_vars(fit, max_print = 400)

# If you want the quick plots for the worst parameters:
trace_worst(fit, key_summary, top_n = 12)
```

```{r}
# If you want to dig into specific parameters that are showing issues, you can pull their draws and summarise by chain:
bad_pars <- c("sigma_C_obs[2]","sigma_C_obs[6]","sigma_C_obs[7]","sigma_analyte","tau_log_sigma_C_obs")

draws <- as_draws_df(fit$draws(variables = bad_pars))

draws %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = all_of(bad_pars), names_to = "param", values_to = "value") %>%
  group_by(param, .chain) %>%
  summarise(
    median = median(value),
    q25 = quantile(value, 0.25),
    q75 = quantile(value, 0.75),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(param, .chain) %>%
  print(n = Inf)
```


```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
# Takes a long time to run (2-3+ hours)
#cmdstan_dashboard(fit, vars = vars_important, trank = TRUE)
cmdstan_dashboard(fit)

# Save a static dashboard image for later review outside the knitted HTML.
# This is non-fatal because some graphics devices or cmdstanr versions may
# behave differently across machines.
dashboard_png <- file.path("out_cmdstanr", paste0("cmdstan_dashboard_", model_version, ".png"))
tryCatch({
  grDevices::png(filename = dashboard_png, width = 2400, height = 1800, res = 220)
  cmdstan_dashboard(fit)
  grDevices::dev.off()
  message("Saved cmdstan dashboard PNG to: ", dashboard_png)
}, error = function(e) {
  if (grDevices::dev.cur() > 1) {
    try(grDevices::dev.off(), silent = TRUE)
  }
  message("Could not save cmdstan dashboard PNG: ", conditionMessage(e))
})

```



```{r stan analyte effects}
## =====================================================================
## Extract posterior samples from cmdstanr into a "rethinking-style" list
## DROP-IN replacement for v1p8 (NO fit$summary; uses fit$draws only)
##
## Core fix vs your failing attempt:
##   cmdstanr/posterior store indexed names like alpha[1], not base "alpha",
##   so we must request indexed variable names explicitly.
##
## v1p8 specifics:
##   - Residue crop effect is by *previous crop*: gamma_PCr_res, sigma_PCr_res
##     (we alias to gamma_Cr_res / sigma_Cr_res for downstream compatibility)
## =====================================================================

dat_used <- if (exists("stan_dat_cmd")) stan_dat_cmd else stan_dat

# ---- Dimensions ----
A_n   <- dat_used$A_n
B_n   <- dat_used$B_n
S_n   <- dat_used$S_n
F_n   <- dat_used$F_n
Y_n   <- dat_used$Y_n
Cr_n  <- dat_used$Cr_n
N     <- dat_used$N
PCr_n <- if (!is.null(dat_used$PrevCr_n)) dat_used$PrevCr_n else Cr_n  # v1p8 residue groups

# Residue placeholder check (all-constant means no supported effect yet)
res_sd <- suppressWarnings(sd(dat_used$RES, na.rm = TRUE))
res_is_placeholder <- is.na(res_sd) || res_sd == 0

# ---- Helpers to build CmdStan variable names (indexed) ----
vnames_vec <- function(name, n) {
  paste0(name, "[", seq_len(n), "]")
}
vnames_mat <- function(name, n1, n2) {
  # CmdStan column order is typically: [1,1],[2,1],...,[n1,1],[1,2],...,[n1,n2]
  out <- character(n1 * n2)
  k <- 1L
  for (j in seq_len(n2)) {
    for (i in seq_len(n1)) {
      out[k] <- paste0(name, "[", i, ",", j, "]")
      k <- k + 1L
    }
  }
  out
}
vnames_3d <- function(name, n1, n2, n3) {
  # Typical order: i fastest, then j, then k
  out <- character(n1 * n2 * n3)
  k <- 1L
  for (kk in seq_len(n3)) {
    for (jj in seq_len(n2)) {
      for (ii in seq_len(n1)) {
        out[k] <- paste0(name, "[", ii, ",", jj, ",", kk, "]")
        k <- k + 1L
      }
    }
  }
  out
}
vnames_2d_y_a <- function(name, Y_n, A_n) {
  # F_year is declared as matrix[Y_n, A_n]
  vnames_mat(name, Y_n, A_n)
}

# ---- Build variable list to pull (indexed + scalars) ----
vars_indexed <- c(
  # analyte-level effects (vectors length A_n)
  vnames_vec("alpha",      A_n),
  vnames_vec("beta_stir",  A_n),
  vnames_vec("beta_cin",   A_n),
  vnames_vec("beta_dup",   A_n),
  vnames_vec("beta_res_C", A_n),

  # random effects (matrices)
  vnames_mat("gamma_B", A_n, B_n),
  vnames_mat("gamma_S", A_n, S_n),
  vnames_mat("gamma_F", A_n, F_n),

  # crop effects
  vnames_mat("gamma_Cr", A_n, Cr_n),
  vnames_vec("gamma_Cr_V", Cr_n),

  # irrigation effect by analyte (vector)
  vnames_vec("beta_irr", A_n),

  # GP year effect: matrix[Y_n, A_n]
  vnames_2d_y_a("F_year", Y_n, A_n),

  # multi-output GP Cholesky correlation matrix (A_n x A_n)
  vnames_mat("L_corr_Agp", A_n, A_n),

  # multi-output GP SDs (vector)
  vnames_vec("sigma_Agp", A_n),

  # concentration SD structure
  vnames_vec("sigma_C_obs", A_n),
  vnames_vec("z_log_sigma_C_obs", A_n),
  
  # random-effect SDs (needed for SD diagnostics)
  vnames_vec("sigma_A", 4),
  vnames_vec("sigma_B", B_n),
  vnames_vec("sigma_S", S_n),
  vnames_vec("sigma_F", F_n),

  # latent states (length N) used by downstream plots
  vnames_vec("mu_C",    N),
  vnames_vec("C_true",  N),
  vnames_vec("mu_V",    N),
  vnames_vec("V_true",  N),
  vnames_vec("mu_res",  N),
  vnames_vec("RES_star",N),

  # v1p8 residue crop grouping (PrevCr)
  vnames_vec("gamma_PCr_res", PCr_n)
)

vars_scalars <- c(
  # volume / other slopes
  "a_V", "b_V", "beta_vol", "beta_vin", "beta_res_V",
  "mu_beta_irr", "sigma_V", "sigma_VOL_obs",

  # irrigation hierarchy (vector sigma_beta_irr + Cholesky L_beta_irr)
  # sigma_beta_irr is vector length A_n; L_beta_irr is matrix A_n x A_n (Cholesky)
  # (these are used in some diagnostics/plots; harmless to include)
  vnames_vec("sigma_beta_irr", A_n),
  vnames_mat("L_beta_irr", A_n, A_n),

  # crop SDs
  vnames_vec("sigma_Cr", A_n),
  "sigma_Cr_V",

  # GP hyperparams
  "etasq_year", "rhosq_year",

  # concentration SD hyperparams
  "sigma_analyte", "mu_log_sigma_C_obs", "tau_log_sigma_C_obs",

  # residue submodel scalars
  "res_base", "b_res_stir", "sigma_PCr_res", "sigma_res_obs"
)

# Flatten (vars_scalars contains indexed names too)
vars_to_pull <- c(vars_indexed, vars_scalars)

# ---- Pull draws (NO fit$summary) ----
# If you ever want to reduce memory: set max_draws <- 500 and subsample posterior draws
draws_mat <- fit$draws(variables = vars_to_pull, format = "matrix")
n_draws <- nrow(draws_mat)

# ---- Utilities to retrieve indexed/scalar params from draws_mat ----
get_param_mat <- function(draws_mat, par) {
  sel <- grepl(paste0("^", par, "\\["), colnames(draws_mat))
  if (!any(sel)) stop("No indexed columns found for parameter '", par, "'.")
  draws_mat[, sel, drop = FALSE]
}
get_param_vec <- function(draws_mat, par) {
  if (!(par %in% colnames(draws_mat))) stop("No scalar column found for parameter '", par, "'.")
  draws_mat[, par]
}

# ---- analyte-level matrices (draws × A_n)
alpha_mat      <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "alpha")
beta_stir_mat  <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_stir")
beta_cin_mat   <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_cin")
beta_dup_mat   <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_dup")
beta_res_C_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_res_C")

# ---- analyte × block random effects: draws × A_n × B_n
gamma_B_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_B")
gamma_B_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, B_n))
k <- 1L
for (b in seq_len(B_n)) for (a in seq_len(A_n)) { gamma_B_arr[, a, b] <- gamma_B_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- analyte × sampler random effects: draws × A_n × S_n
gamma_S_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_S")
gamma_S_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, S_n))
k <- 1L
for (s in seq_len(S_n)) for (a in seq_len(A_n)) { gamma_S_arr[, a, s] <- gamma_S_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- analyte × flume random effects: draws × A_n × F_n
gamma_F_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_F")
gamma_F_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, F_n))
k <- 1L
for (f in seq_len(F_n)) for (a in seq_len(A_n)) { gamma_F_arr[, a, f] <- gamma_F_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- GP year effects: draws × Y_n × A_n
F_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "F_year")
F_array <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, Y_n, A_n))
k <- 1L
for (a in seq_len(A_n)) for (y in seq_len(Y_n)) { F_array[, y, a] <- F_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- crop effects on concentration: gamma_Cr[A, Cr]
gamma_Cr_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_Cr")
if (ncol(gamma_Cr_mat) != A_n * Cr_n) {
  stop("gamma_Cr dimension mismatch: expected ", A_n * Cr_n, " columns but found ", ncol(gamma_Cr_mat), ".")
}
gamma_Cr_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, Cr_n))
k <- 1L
for (cr in seq_len(Cr_n)) for (a in seq_len(A_n)) { gamma_Cr_arr[, a, cr] <- gamma_Cr_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- crop effects on volume: gamma_Cr_V[Cr]
gamma_Cr_V_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_Cr_V")
if (ncol(gamma_Cr_V_mat) != Cr_n) {
  stop("gamma_Cr_V dimension mismatch: expected ", Cr_n, " columns but found ", ncol(gamma_Cr_V_mat), ".")
}

# ---- volume + irrigation terms
beta_vol_vec      <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "beta_vol")
beta_vin_vec      <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "beta_vin")
beta_irr_mat      <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_irr")
a_V_vec           <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "a_V")
b_V_vec           <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "b_V")
sigma_V_vec       <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_V")
sigma_VOL_obs_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_VOL_obs")
beta_res_V_vec    <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "beta_res_V")
mu_beta_irr_vec   <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "mu_beta_irr")

# ---- sigma objects (v1p8)
sigma_analyte_vec        <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_analyte")
sigma_C_obs_mat          <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_C_obs")
mu_log_sigma_C_obs_vec   <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "mu_log_sigma_C_obs")
tau_log_sigma_C_obs_vec  <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "tau_log_sigma_C_obs")
z_log_sigma_C_obs_mat    <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "z_log_sigma_C_obs")
sigma_Cr_mat             <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_Cr")
sigma_Cr_V_vec           <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_Cr_V")
etasq_year_vec           <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "etasq_year")
rhosq_year_vec           <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "rhosq_year")

# ---- latent states used downstream
mu_C_mat    <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "mu_C")
C_true_mat  <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "C_true")
mu_V_mat    <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "mu_V")
V_true_mat  <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "V_true")
mu_res_mat  <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "mu_res")
RES_star_mat<- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "RES_star")

# ---- residue submodel (v1p8: previous crop)
res_base_vec      <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "res_base")
b_res_stir_vec    <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "b_res_stir")
gamma_PCr_res_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_PCr_res")  # draws × PCr_n
sigma_PCr_res_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_PCr_res")
sigma_res_obs_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_res_obs")

# Alias for downstream chunks that expect "Cr"-named residue group effects
gamma_Cr_res_mat <- gamma_PCr_res_mat
sigma_Cr_res_vec <- sigma_PCr_res_vec

# ---- Assemble posterior list (keep names used by v1p7p5 downstream code)
post <- list(
  alpha         = alpha_mat,
  beta_stir     = beta_stir_mat,
  beta_cin      = beta_cin_mat,
  beta_dup      = beta_dup_mat,
  beta_res_C    = beta_res_C_mat,

  gamma_B       = gamma_B_arr,
  gamma_S       = gamma_S_arr,
  gamma_F       = gamma_F_arr,

  gamma_Cr      = gamma_Cr_arr,       # draws × A_n × Cr_n
  gamma_Cr_V    = gamma_Cr_V_mat,     # draws × Cr_n

  beta_vol      = beta_vol_vec,
  beta_vin      = beta_vin_vec,
  beta_irr      = beta_irr_mat,
  mu_beta_irr   = mu_beta_irr_vec,
  a_V           = a_V_vec,
  b_V           = b_V_vec,
  sigma_V       = sigma_V_vec,
  sigma_VOL_obs = sigma_VOL_obs_vec,
  beta_res_V    = beta_res_V_vec,

  # C model SDs
  sigma_analyte = sigma_analyte_vec,
  sigma_C_obs   = sigma_C_obs_mat,
  mu_log_sigma_C_obs  = mu_log_sigma_C_obs_vec,
  tau_log_sigma_C_obs = tau_log_sigma_C_obs_vec,
  z_log_sigma_C_obs   = z_log_sigma_C_obs_mat,

  # crop SDs
  sigma_Cr      = sigma_Cr_mat,
  sigma_Cr_V    = sigma_Cr_V_vec,

  # GP
  F_year        = F_array,
  etasq_year    = etasq_year_vec,
  rhosq_year    = rhosq_year_vec,

  # latent states used by plots
  mu_C          = mu_C_mat,
  C_true        = C_true_mat,
  mu_V          = mu_V_mat,
  V_true        = V_true_mat,
  mu_res        = mu_res_mat,
  RES_star      = RES_star_mat,

  # residue submodel (aliased to legacy names)
  res_base      = res_base_vec,
  b_res_stir    = b_res_stir_vec,
  gamma_Cr_res  = gamma_Cr_res_mat,
  sigma_Cr_res  = sigma_Cr_res_vec,
  sigma_res_obs = sigma_res_obs_vec
)

#draws_mat <- pull_draws_mat(vars_to_pull)
n_draws <- nrow(draws_mat)

# ---- Helpers to extract scalars / indexed params ----
get_param_mat <- function(dm, par) {
  sel <- grepl(paste0("^", par, "\\["), colnames(dm))
  if (!any(sel)) stop("No indexed columns found for parameter '", par, "'.")
  dm[, sel, drop = FALSE]
}
get_param_vec <- function(dm, par) {
  if (!(par %in% colnames(dm))) stop("No scalar column found for parameter '", par, "'.")
  dm[, par]
}

# ---- Analyte-level (draws × A_n) ----
alpha_mat      <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "alpha")
beta_stir_mat  <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_stir")
beta_cin_mat   <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_cin")
beta_dup_mat   <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_dup")
beta_res_C_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_res_C")

# ---- Random effects: gamma_B[A,B], gamma_S[A,S], gamma_F[A,F] ----
gamma_B_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_B")
gamma_B_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, B_n))
k <- 1L
for (b in seq_len(B_n)) for (a in seq_len(A_n)) { gamma_B_arr[, a, b] <- gamma_B_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

gamma_S_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_S")
gamma_S_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, S_n))
k <- 1L
for (s in seq_len(S_n)) for (a in seq_len(A_n)) { gamma_S_arr[, a, s] <- gamma_S_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

gamma_F_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_F")
gamma_F_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, F_n))
k <- 1L
for (f in seq_len(F_n)) for (a in seq_len(A_n)) { gamma_F_arr[, a, f] <- gamma_F_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- GP year effects: F_year[Y,A] ----
F_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "F_year")
F_array <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, Y_n, A_n))
k <- 1L
for (a in seq_len(A_n)) for (y in seq_len(Y_n)) { F_array[, y, a] <- F_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- Crop effects on concentration: gamma_Cr[A,Cr] ----
gamma_Cr_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_Cr")
if (ncol(gamma_Cr_mat) != A_n * Cr_n) {
  stop("gamma_Cr dimension mismatch: expected ", A_n * Cr_n, " but found ", ncol(gamma_Cr_mat), ".")
}
gamma_Cr_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, Cr_n))
k <- 1L
for (cr in seq_len(Cr_n)) for (a in seq_len(A_n)) { gamma_Cr_arr[, a, cr] <- gamma_Cr_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- Crop effects on volume: gamma_Cr_V[Cr] ----
gamma_Cr_V_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_Cr_V")
if (ncol(gamma_Cr_V_mat) != Cr_n) {
  stop("gamma_Cr_V dimension mismatch: expected ", Cr_n, " but found ", ncol(gamma_Cr_V_mat), ".")
}

# ---- Irrigation hierarchy: beta_irr[A], plus mu/sigma/L ----
beta_irr_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "beta_irr")
if (ncol(beta_irr_mat) != A_n) {
  stop("beta_irr dimension mismatch: expected ", A_n, " but found ", ncol(beta_irr_mat), ".")
}

mu_beta_irr_vec   <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "mu_beta_irr")
sigma_beta_irr_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_beta_irr")
if (ncol(sigma_beta_irr_mat) != A_n) {
  stop("sigma_beta_irr dimension mismatch: expected ", A_n, " but found ", ncol(sigma_beta_irr_mat), ".")
}

L_beta_irr_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "L_beta_irr")
if (ncol(L_beta_irr_mat) != A_n * A_n) {
  stop("L_beta_irr dimension mismatch: expected ", A_n*A_n, " but found ", ncol(L_beta_irr_mat), ".")
}
L_beta_irr_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, A_n))
k <- 1L
for (j in seq_len(A_n)) for (i in seq_len(A_n)) { L_beta_irr_arr[, i, j] <- L_beta_irr_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- GP correlation + SD ----
sigma_Agp_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_Agp")
if (ncol(sigma_Agp_mat) != A_n) stop("sigma_Agp dimension mismatch: expected ", A_n, " but found ", ncol(sigma_Agp_mat), ".")
L_corr_Agp_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "L_corr_Agp")
if (ncol(L_corr_Agp_mat) != A_n * A_n) stop("L_corr_Agp dimension mismatch: expected ", A_n*A_n, " but found ", ncol(L_corr_Agp_mat), ".")
L_corr_Agp_arr <- array(NA_real_, dim = c(n_draws, A_n, A_n))
k <- 1L
for (j in seq_len(A_n)) for (i in seq_len(A_n)) { L_corr_Agp_arr[, i, j] <- L_corr_Agp_mat[, k]; k <- k + 1L }

# ---- Scalars + SD structures ----
beta_vol_vec       <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "beta_vol")
beta_vin_vec       <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "beta_vin")
a_V_vec            <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "a_V")
b_V_vec            <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "b_V")
sigma_V_vec        <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_V")
sigma_VOL_obs_vec  <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_VOL_obs")
beta_res_V_vec     <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "beta_res_V")

sigma_A_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_A")
sigma_B_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_B")
sigma_S_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_S")
sigma_F_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_F")

sigma_Cr_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_Cr")
sigma_Cr_V_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_Cr_V")

etasq_year_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "etasq_year")
rhosq_year_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "rhosq_year")

sigma_analyte_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_analyte")
sigma_C_obs_mat   <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "sigma_C_obs")
mu_log_sigma_C_obs_vec  <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "mu_log_sigma_C_obs")
tau_log_sigma_C_obs_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "tau_log_sigma_C_obs")
z_log_sigma_C_obs_mat   <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "z_log_sigma_C_obs")

# ---- Latent states (draws × N) ----
mu_C_mat   <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "mu_C")
C_true_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "C_true")
mu_V_mat   <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "mu_V")
V_true_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "V_true")
mu_res_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "mu_res")
RES_star_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "RES_star")

# ---- Residue submodel (v1p8: previous crop) ----
res_base_vec   <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "res_base")
b_res_stir_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "b_res_stir")
gamma_PCr_res_mat <- get_param_mat(draws_mat, "gamma_PCr_res")
if (ncol(gamma_PCr_res_mat) != PCr_n) {
  stop("gamma_PCr_res dimension mismatch: expected ", PCr_n, " but found ", ncol(gamma_PCr_res_mat), ".")
}
sigma_PCr_res_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_PCr_res")
sigma_res_obs_vec <- get_param_vec(draws_mat, "sigma_res_obs")

# ---- Assemble posterior list (keep v1p7p5 names where downstream code expects them) ----
post <- list(
  # concentration fixed effects
  alpha      = alpha_mat,
  beta_stir  = beta_stir_mat,
  beta_cin   = beta_cin_mat,
  beta_dup   = beta_dup_mat,
  beta_res_C = beta_res_C_mat,

  # random effects
  gamma_B = gamma_B_arr,
  gamma_S = gamma_S_arr,
  gamma_F = gamma_F_arr,

  # crop effects
  gamma_Cr   = gamma_Cr_arr,     # draws × A × Cr
  gamma_Cr_V = gamma_Cr_V_mat,   # draws × Cr

  # volume / irrigation
  beta_vol      = beta_vol_vec,
  beta_vin      = beta_vin_vec,
  beta_irr      = beta_irr_mat,
  mu_beta_irr   = mu_beta_irr_vec,
  sigma_beta_irr = sigma_beta_irr_mat,
  L_beta_irr    = L_beta_irr_arr,
  a_V           = a_V_vec,
  b_V           = b_V_vec,
  sigma_V       = sigma_V_vec,
  sigma_VOL_obs = sigma_VOL_obs_vec,
  beta_res_V    = beta_res_V_vec,

  # SDs for group effects
  sigma_A = sigma_A_mat,
  sigma_B = sigma_B_mat,
  sigma_S = sigma_S_mat,
  sigma_F = sigma_F_mat,

  # concentration SD structure
  sigma_analyte = sigma_analyte_vec,
  sigma_C_obs   = sigma_C_obs_mat,
  mu_log_sigma_C_obs  = mu_log_sigma_C_obs_vec,
  tau_log_sigma_C_obs = tau_log_sigma_C_obs_vec,
  z_log_sigma_C_obs   = z_log_sigma_C_obs_mat,

  # crop SDs
  sigma_Cr   = sigma_Cr_mat,
  sigma_Cr_V = sigma_Cr_V_vec,

  # GP
  F_year      = F_array,
  etasq_year  = etasq_year_vec,
  rhosq_year  = rhosq_year_vec,
  sigma_Agp   = sigma_Agp_mat,
  L_corr_Agp  = L_corr_Agp_arr,

  # latent states
  mu_C    = mu_C_mat,
  C_true  = C_true_mat,
  mu_V    = mu_V_mat,
  V_true  = V_true_mat,
  mu_res  = mu_res_mat,
  RES_star = RES_star_mat,

  # residue submodel (v1p8-native names)
  res_base      = res_base_vec,
  b_res_stir    = b_res_stir_vec,
  gamma_PCr_res = gamma_PCr_res_mat,
  sigma_PCr_res = sigma_PCr_res_vec,
  sigma_res_obs = sigma_res_obs_vec,

  # backwards-compatible aliases (so older plotting chunks still work)
  gamma_Cr_res  = gamma_PCr_res_mat,
  sigma_Cr_res  = sigma_PCr_res_vec,

  # residue placeholder flag for downstream summaries
  res_is_placeholder = res_is_placeholder
)

# Quick sanity print (fast, no summary())
cat("\n[v1p8] post assembled.\n")
cat("  draws:", n_draws, " A_n:", A_n, " Y_n:", Y_n, " Cr_n:", Cr_n, " PrevCr_n:", PCr_n, "\n")
cat("  residue placeholder:", post$res_is_placeholder, "\n")
```


## 5a. Posterior effects of residue and crop

The v1p8 model includes (i) an analyte-specific residue slope on concentration (`beta_res_C[A]`), (ii) a scalar residue slope on volume (`beta_res_V`), (iii) analyte-specific crop intercepts on concentration (`gamma_Cr[A,Cr]`), and (iv) crop intercepts on volume (`gamma_Cr_V[Cr]`). The plots below provide quick, publication-ready summaries of these terms.

```{r residue effects on concentration}
# Residue effect on concentration by analyte
# ---- Prepare posterior draws ----
post_resC_df <- as.data.frame(post$beta_res_C)
colnames(post_resC_df) <- as.character(analyte_lookup$analyte_abbr)

post_resC_long <- post_resC_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "analyte_abbr",
    values_to = "beta_resC"
  )

# ---- Reorder analytes by posterior median (base R, no forcats) ----
analyte_order <- post_resC_long %>%
  group_by(analyte_abbr) %>%
  summarise(med = median(beta_resC)) %>%
  arrange(med) %>%
  pull(analyte_abbr)

post_resC_long$analyte_abbr <- factor(
  post_resC_long$analyte_abbr,
  levels = analyte_order
)

# ---- Ridgeline plot ----
p_resC_ridge <- ggplot(post_resC_long, aes(x = beta_resC, y = analyte_abbr)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, linewidth=1) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    fill = "darkred",
    alpha = 0.55,
    scale = 1.1,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles = 2,
    quantile_color = "black",
    quantile_linetype = 2,
    quantile_linewidth = 1,
    bandwidth = 0.02
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Residue effect on concentration",
    y = "Analyte",
    #title = "Posterior residue effects on concentration (v1p8)",
    subtitle = if (res_is_placeholder)
      "Residue is currently a placeholder (all zeros), effects are not identifiable"
      else NULL
  ) +
  theme_ridges(grid = TRUE, center_axis_labels = TRUE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    text = element_text(size = 14)
  )

p_resC_ridge

ggsave(
  filename = "../figs/residue_effect_on_concentration_by_analyte_v1p8.jpg",
  plot = p_resC_ridge,
  width = 10,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300
)

```

```{r residue effect on volume}
# Residue effect on volume (scalar slope) posterior density
# If placeholder, report and plot a spike at zero.

b_resV <- if (isTRUE(res_is_placeholder)) rep(0, 200) else post$beta_res_V

dens <- stats::density(b_resV)

plot(
  dens,
  type = "l",
  lwd  = 3,
  col  = "darkred",
  xlab = "Residue effect on Volume",
  ylab = "Density",
  main = "Posterior residue effect on volume",
  bty  = "l",
  # ensure xlim always shows zero
  xlim = range(c(dens$x, 0))
)
abline(v = 0, lty = 2)

jpeg("../figs/residue_effect_on_volume_density_v1p8.jpeg", width = 2000, height = 1300, res = 200)
plot(
  dens,
  type = "l",
  lwd  = 3,
  col  = "darkred",
  xlab = "Residue effect on Volume",
  ylab = "Density",
  main = "Posterior residue effect on volume",
  bty  = "l",
  # ensure xlim always shows zero
  xlim = range(c(dens$x, 0))
)
abline(v = 0, lty = 2)
dev.off()
```

```{r crop effects on concentration}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Crop effects on concentration: posterior ridgelines by crop
# post$gamma_Cr is draws × A_n × Cr_n
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Build a long draw table: one row per (draw, analyte, crop)
crop_levels <- as.character(crop_lookup$Crop)
analyte_levels <- as.character(analyte_lookup$analyte_abbr)

cropC_long <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(Cr_n), function(cr) {
  m <- post$gamma_Cr[, , cr, drop = FALSE]      # draws × A × 1
  m2 <- m[, , 1]                               # draws × A
  df <- as.data.frame(m2)
  colnames(df) <- analyte_levels
  df %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = everything(),
      names_to = "analyte_abbr",
      values_to = "crop_eff"
    ) %>%
    mutate(Crop = crop_levels[cr])
}))

# Make Crop a factor with your desired ordering
cropC_long$Crop <- factor(cropC_long$Crop, levels = crop_levels)

# Choose ONE analyte order used in every facet (recommended for cross-panel comparison):
analyte_order_global <- cropC_long %>%
  group_by(analyte_abbr) %>%
  summarise(med = median(crop_eff), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(med) %>%
  pull(analyte_abbr)

cropC_long$analyte_abbr <- factor(cropC_long$analyte_abbr, levels = analyte_order_global)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# NEW: pick a tighter x-axis range from posterior quantiles
# - uses pooled draws across all analytes/crops
# - keeps 0 inside the view
# ------------------------------------------------------------
q_lo <- unname(quantile(cropC_long$crop_eff, probs = 0.005, na.rm = TRUE))
q_hi <- unname(quantile(cropC_long$crop_eff, probs = 0.995, na.rm = TRUE))

# symmetric range around 0, with a small pad so ridges aren't clipped
half_range <- max(abs(c(q_lo, q_hi)), 0.05)   # 0.05 is a safety floor
pad <- 0.05 * half_range
x_lim <- c(-half_range - pad, half_range + pad)

# ---- Faceted ridgeline plot ----
p_cropC_ridge <- ggplot(cropC_long, aes(x = crop_eff, y = analyte_abbr)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    fill = "darkred",
    alpha = 0.55,
    scale = 1.05,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles = 2,
    quantile_color = "black",
    quantile_linetype = 2,
    quantile_linewidth = 0.9,
    bandwidth = 0.02
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Crop, ncol = 3, scales = "fixed") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = x_lim) +
  labs(
    x = "Crop effect on concentration",
    y = "Analyte",
    subtitle = "Posterior distributions of analyte-specific crop intercepts"
  ) +
  theme_ridges(grid = TRUE, center_axis_labels = TRUE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    text = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9)
  )

p_cropC_ridge

ggsave(
  filename = "../figs/crop_effects_on_concentration_by_analyte_v1p8.jpg",
  plot = p_cropC_ridge,
  width = 9,
  height = 12,
  dpi = 300
)

```

```{r crop effects on volume}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Crop effects on volume: ridgeline posterior by crop
# post$gamma_Cr_V is draws × Cr_n
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Long posterior draw table
post_cropV_df <- as.data.frame(post$gamma_Cr_V)
colnames(post_cropV_df) <- as.character(crop_lookup$Crop)

post_cropV_long <- post_cropV_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "Crop",
    values_to = "crop_eff_V"
  )

# Reorder crops by posterior median (base R, no forcats)
crop_order <- post_cropV_long %>%
  group_by(Crop) %>%
  summarise(med = median(crop_eff_V), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(med) %>%
  pull(Crop)

post_cropV_long$Crop <- factor(post_cropV_long$Crop, levels = crop_order)

# Ridgeline plot
p_cropV_ridge <- ggplot(post_cropV_long, aes(x = crop_eff_V, y = Crop)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, linewidth = 1) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    fill = "darkred",
    alpha = 0.55,
    scale = 1.1,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles = 2,
    quantile_color = "black",
    quantile_linetype = 2,
    quantile_linewidth = 1,
    bandwidth = 0.02
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Crop effect on volume",
    y = "Crop",
    #title = "Posterior crop effects on volume"
  ) +
  theme_ridges(grid = TRUE, center_axis_labels = TRUE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    text = element_text(size = 14)
  )

p_cropV_ridge

ggsave(
  filename = "../figs/crop_effects_on_volume_v1p8.jpg",
  plot = p_cropV_ridge,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

```



---



# 4.5 Plot posterior slopes for irrigation-count effects on concentration

```{r irrigation effects on conc}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Irrigation-count effects on concentration by analyte
# beta_irr_mat is draws × A_n (MVN prior across analytes in v1p8)
# Note: Stan standardizes IRR internally (IRR_z), so beta_irr is per +1 SD(IRR)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Long posterior draw table
post_irr_df <- as.data.frame(beta_irr_mat)
colnames(post_irr_df) <- as.character(analytes_keep)

post_irr_long <- post_irr_df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "Analyte",
    values_to = "beta_irr"
  )

# Reorder analytes by posterior median (base R, no forcats)
analyte_order <- post_irr_long %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Analyte) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(med = median(beta_irr), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(med) %>%
  dplyr::pull(Analyte)

post_irr_long$Analyte <- factor(post_irr_long$Analyte, levels = analyte_order)

# Ridgeline plot (match crop style)
p_irr_ridge <- ggplot(post_irr_long, aes(x = beta_irr, y = Analyte)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, linewidth = 1) +
  ggridges::stat_density_ridges(
    fill = "darkred",
    alpha = 0.55,
    scale = 1.1,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles = 2,
    quantile_color = "black",
    quantile_linetype = 2,
    quantile_linewidth = 1,
    bandwidth = 0.02
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Effect of Irrigation Number on Concentration",
    y = "Analyte"
    # title = "Posterior irrigation-number effects on concentration"
  ) +
  theme_ridges(grid = TRUE, center_axis_labels = TRUE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    text = element_text(size = 14)
  )

p_irr_ridge

ggsave(
  filename = "../figs/irr_effects_on_concentration_v1p8.jpg",
  plot = p_irr_ridge,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

```


# 5. Plot posterior slopes for STIR effects on concentration

```{r stir effects on conc}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STIR effects on concentration by analyte: posterior ridgelines
# Uses post$beta_stir (draws × A_n)
# Overwrites: ../figs/load1p8_STIReffects.jpeg
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# ---- Prepare posterior draws ----
post_stir_df <- as.data.frame(post$beta_stir)
colnames(post_stir_df) <- as.character(analyte_lookup$analyte_abbr)

post_stir_long <- post_stir_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "analyte_abbr",
    values_to = "beta_stir"
  )

# ---- Order analytes by posterior mean (base R, no forcats) ----
analyte_order <- post_stir_long %>%
  group_by(analyte_abbr) %>%
  summarise(mu = mean(beta_stir), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(mu) %>%
  pull(analyte_abbr)

post_stir_long$analyte_abbr <- factor(
  post_stir_long$analyte_abbr,
  levels = analyte_order
)

# ---- Ridgeline plot ----
p_stir_ridge <- ggplot(post_stir_long, aes(x = beta_stir, y = analyte_abbr)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, linewidth = 1) +
  stat_density_ridges(
    fill = "darkred",
    alpha = 0.55,
    scale = 1.1,
    rel_min_height = 0.01,
    quantile_lines = TRUE,
    quantiles = 2,
    quantile_color = "black",
    quantile_linetype = 2,
    quantile_linewidth = 1,
    bandwidth = 0.01
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Effect of STIR on Concentration",
    y = "Analyte (ordered by mean effect)",
    title = "Analyte-specific STIR effects with posterior medians"
  ) +
  theme_ridges(grid = TRUE, center_axis_labels = TRUE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    text = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )

p_stir_ridge

# ---- Save (overwrite existing) ----
ggsave(
  filename = "../figs/load1p8_STIReffects.jpeg",
  plot = p_stir_ridge,
  width = 9,
  height = 9,
  dpi = 300
)

```

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STIR effects on volume
# ------------------------------------------------------------

```{r stir effects on volume}
plot_stir_volume_effect <- function(fit, param = "b_V",
                                    file = NULL,
                                    main = "Posterior effect of STIR on runoff volume",
                                    xlab = "Effect of STIR on runoff volume",
                                    ylab = "Density") {

  if (inherits(fit, "CmdStanMCMC")) {
    draws <- fit$draws(variables = param, format = "matrix")
    if (!(param %in% colnames(draws))) {
      stop("Parameter '", param, "' not found in cmdstan draws.")
    }
    b <- as.numeric(draws[, param])
  } else if (inherits(fit, "stanfit")) {
    s <- rstan::extract(fit, pars = param, permuted = TRUE)
    if (!(param %in% names(s))) stop("Parameter '", param, "' not found in stanfit.")
    b <- as.numeric(s[[param]])
  } else if (is.numeric(fit)) {
    b <- as.numeric(fit)
  } else {
    stop("Unsupported object type for 'fit'. Use a CmdStanMCMC, stanfit, or numeric vector.")
  }

  b <- b[is.finite(b)]

  if (!is.null(file)) {
    jpeg(file, width = 2000, height = 1300, res = 200)
    on.exit(dev.off(), add = TRUE)
  }

  dens <- stats::density(b)

  plot(
    dens,
    type = "l",
    lwd  = 4,
    col  = "darkred",
    xlab = xlab,
    ylab = ylab,
    main = main,
    bty  = "l",
    xlim = range(c(dens$x, 0))
  )

  abline(v = 0, lty = 2)

  invisible(b)
}

# Plot to device
plot_stir_volume_effect(fit, param = "b_V")

# Save to file (v1p8 filename)
plot_stir_volume_effect(
  fit,
  param = "b_V",
  file = "../figs/v1p8_post_STIR_effect_on_volume.jpg"
)
```

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# STIR effects on residue
# ------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
# STIR effect on residue (logit-normal submodel)
# Scalar slope: b_res_stir

b_res_stir <- post$b_res_stir

dens <- stats::density(b_res_stir)

# ---- Plot to Rmd output ----
plot(
  dens,
  type = "l",
  lwd  = 3,
  col  = "darkred",
  xlab = "STIR effect on logit(Residue proportion)",
  ylab = "Density",
  main = "Posterior STIR effect on residue",
  bty  = "l",
  # ensure zero is always visible
  xlim = range(c(dens$x, 0))
)

abline(v = 0, lty = 2)

# ---- Save identical plot to file ----
jpeg("../figs/stir_effect_on_residue_density_v1p8.jpeg",
     width = 2000, height = 1300, res = 200)

plot(
  dens,
  type = "l",
  lwd  = 3,
  col  = "darkred",
  xlab = "STIR effect on logit(Residue proportion)",
  ylab = "Density",
  main = "Posterior STIR effect on residue",
  bty  = "l",
  xlim = range(c(dens$x, 0))
)

abline(v = 0, lty = 2)

dev.off()
```


## 6. Generative STIR → Load curves by analyte --------------------------------

```{r load-generative-curves}
## Scenario-based STIR -> Load link + posterior predictive curves ----------
## v1p8 (dual ribbons):
##   1) "mean" ribbon = uncertainty in expected mean relationship (link-style)
##   2) "pp" ribbon   = posterior predictive including latent process + obs noise (sim-style)
##
## Notes:
## - Uses sigma_V + sigma_VOL_obs for volume (latent + obs)
## - Uses sigma_analyte + sigma_C_obs[A] for concentration (latent + obs)
## - Residue submodel is NOT used; RES is treated as an input covariate.
## - v1p8: irrigation effect is analyte-specific and IRR is standardized (IRR_z).

# 1. Back-transform stats (only computed once)
c_stats <- d_mod %>%
  dplyr::group_by(A) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    c_mean = mean(Result_mg_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    c_sd   = sd(Result_mg_L,   na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

V_mean <- mean(d_mod$Volume, na.rm = TRUE)
V_sd   <- sd(d_mod$Volume, na.rm = TRUE)

# STIR raw mean/sd for Season_STIR_toDate
stir_mean <- mean(d_mod$Season_STIR_toDate, na.rm = TRUE)
stir_sd   <- sd(d_mod$Season_STIR_toDate, na.rm = TRUE)

# Empirical means for averaging when left NULL
IRR_bar_raw <- mean(d_mod$irr_num, na.rm = TRUE)
DUP_bar     <- mean(d_mod$dup,     na.rm = TRUE)

# v1p8: IRR standardization to match Stan IRR_z
irr_mean <- mean(d_mod$irr_num, na.rm = TRUE)
irr_sd   <- sd(d_mod$irr_num,   na.rm = TRUE)
if (!is.finite(irr_sd) || irr_sd == 0) irr_sd <- 1
IRR_bar_z <- (IRR_bar_raw - irr_mean) / irr_sd

# Empirical mean residue proportion when res_z is NULL
RES_bar <- if ("RES_input" %in% names(d_mod)) {
  mean(d_mod$RES_input, na.rm = TRUE)
} else if ("RES" %in% names(d_mod)) {
  mean(d_mod$RES, na.rm = TRUE)
} else if ("residue_prop" %in% names(d_mod)) {
  mean(d_mod$residue_prop, na.rm = TRUE)
} else if ("RES_z" %in% names(d_mod)) {
  mean(d_mod$RES_z, na.rm = TRUE)
} else {
  NA_real_
}

if (!is.finite(RES_bar)) stop("Could not compute RES_bar: d_mod needs RES_input, RES, residue_prop, or RES_z as a residue proportion in (0,1).")
if (RES_bar <= 0 || RES_bar >= 1) stop("RES_bar is not in (0,1). Check that residue is stored as a proportion, not percent.")

# Crop weights for "average over crops" scenario
crop_wts <- d_mod %>%
  dplyr::count(Cr, name = "n") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(w = n / sum(n)) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Cr)

simulate_load_curve_single <- function(
  post,
  analyte,
  STIR_raw_seq = seq(0, 300, length.out = 60),
  block      = NULL,
  sampler    = NULL,
  flume      = NULL,
  irrigation = NULL,
  duplicate  = NULL,
  crop       = NULL,
  res_z      = NULL,   # NULL -> RES_bar; 0 -> 1e-4; (0,1) fixed proportion
  cin_z      = 0,
  year       = NULL,
  n_draws    = 2000,
  ci_prob    = 0.95
) {

  if (!is.numeric(ci_prob) || length(ci_prob) != 1L || ci_prob <= 0 || ci_prob >= 1) {
    stop("ci_prob must be a single numeric value strictly between 0 and 1.")
  }

  # ---- required sigmas in post (fail fast if missing) ----
  need <- c("sigma_V", "sigma_VOL_obs", "sigma_analyte")
  miss <- setdiff(need, names(post))
  if (length(miss) > 0) stop("post is missing required parameter(s): ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "))

  # concentration obs sd is analyte-specific
  if (!("sigma_C_obs" %in% names(post))) {
    stop("post is missing sigma_C_obs (analyte-specific concentration observation SD).")
  }

  # Map analyte label -> index A
  j_idx <- analyte_lookup$A[analyte_lookup$analyte_abbr == analyte]
  if (length(j_idx) != 1L) stop("analyte not found in analyte_lookup")

  # Stats for this analyte
  stats_j  <- dplyr::filter(c_stats, A == j_idx)
  c_mean_j <- stats_j$c_mean
  c_sd_j   <- stats_j$c_sd

  # STIR in z units
  STIR_z_seq <- (STIR_raw_seq - stir_mean) / stir_sd

  # Total draws and subsample index
  n_draws_total <- nrow(post$alpha)
  draw_idx <- if (n_draws_total > n_draws) sample.int(n_draws_total, n_draws) else seq_len(n_draws_total)
  n_s <- length(draw_idx)

  # ---------------- Random effects / covariate settings ----------------

  # Block (Rep)
  if (is.null(block)) {
    gamma_B_draws <- post$gamma_B[draw_idx, j_idx, , drop = FALSE]   # draws × 1 × B_n
    gamma_B_star  <- apply(gamma_B_draws, 1, mean)
    block_label   <- "avg_all_blocks"
  } else {
    B_idx <- block_lookup$B[block_lookup$Rep == block]
    if (length(B_idx) != 1L) stop("block not found in block_lookup")
    gamma_B_star <- post$gamma_B[draw_idx, j_idx, B_idx]
    block_label  <- as.character(block)
  }

  # Sampler method
  if (is.null(sampler)) {
    gamma_S_draws <- post$gamma_S[draw_idx, j_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    gamma_S_star  <- apply(gamma_S_draws, 1, mean)
    sampler_label <- "avg_all_samplers"
  } else {
    S_idx <- sampler_lookup$S[sampler_lookup$SampleMethod == sampler]
    if (length(S_idx) != 1L) stop("sampler not found in sampler_lookup")
    gamma_S_star  <- post$gamma_S[draw_idx, j_idx, S_idx]
    sampler_label <- as.character(sampler)
  }

  # Flume type
  if (is.null(flume)) {
    gamma_F_draws <- post$gamma_F[draw_idx, j_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    gamma_F_star  <- apply(gamma_F_draws, 1, mean)
    flume_label   <- "avg_all_flumes"
  } else {
    Fu_idx <- flume_lookup$Fu[flume_lookup$FlumeMethod == flume]
    if (length(Fu_idx) != 1L) stop("flume not found in flume_lookup")
    gamma_F_star  <- post$gamma_F[draw_idx, j_idx, Fu_idx]
    flume_label   <- as.character(flume)
  }

  # Irrigation (numeric)
  # IMPORTANT: convert raw irrigation count -> IRR_z scale that Stan uses
  if (is.null(irrigation)) {
    IRR_star_raw     <- IRR_bar_raw
    IRR_star_z       <- IRR_bar_z
    irrigation_label <- "avg_all_irrigations"
  } else {
    irr_val <- irrigation_lookup$irr_num[irrigation_lookup$Irrigation == irrigation]
    if (length(irr_val) != 1L) stop("irrigation not found in irrigation_lookup")
    IRR_star_raw     <- irr_val
    IRR_star_z       <- (IRR_star_raw - irr_mean) / irr_sd
    irrigation_label <- as.character(irrigation)
  }

  # Duplicate status (numeric 0/1 or mean)
  if (is.null(duplicate)) {
    DUP_star        <- DUP_bar
    duplicate_label <- "avg_duplicates"
  } else {
    DUP_star        <- as.numeric(duplicate)
    duplicate_label <- ifelse(as.logical(duplicate), "TRUE", "FALSE")
  }

  # Crop: affects BOTH volume and concentration
  if (is.null(crop)) {
    w <- crop_wts$w
    if (length(w) != ncol(post$gamma_Cr_V)) stop("crop weight length != Cr_n for gamma_Cr_V")
    if (length(w) != dim(post$gamma_Cr)[3]) stop("crop weight length != Cr_n for gamma_Cr")

    cropV_draws <- post$gamma_Cr_V[draw_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    gamma_CrV_star <- as.numeric(cropV_draws %*% w)

    cropC_draws <- post$gamma_Cr[draw_idx, j_idx, , drop = FALSE]  # draws × 1 × Cr_n
    cropC_mat   <- matrix(cropC_draws, nrow = n_s, ncol = length(w))
    gamma_CrC_star <- as.numeric(cropC_mat %*% w)

    crop_label <- "avg_over_crops_weighted"
  } else {
    Cr_idx <- crop_lookup$Cr[crop_lookup$Crop == crop]
    if (length(Cr_idx) != 1L) stop("crop not found in crop_lookup (check exact spelling/levels)")
    gamma_CrV_star <- post$gamma_Cr_V[draw_idx, Cr_idx]
    gamma_CrC_star <- post$gamma_Cr[draw_idx, j_idx, Cr_idx]
    crop_label <- as.character(crop)
  }

  # ---------------- Residue handling ----------------
  if (!is.null(res_z) && (length(res_z) != 1L || !is.numeric(res_z))) {
    stop("res_z must be NULL or a single numeric value.")
  }

  if (is.null(res_z)) {
    res_prop <- RES_bar
    res_mode <- "avg_observed"
  } else {
    res_prop <- as.numeric(res_z)
    if (!is.finite(res_prop)) stop("res_z must be finite when not NULL.")
    if (identical(res_prop, 0)) res_prop <- 1e-4
    res_mode <- "fixed"
  }
  if (res_prop <= 0 || res_prop >= 1) stop("Residue proportion must be strictly between 0 and 1. Got: ", res_prop)

  # ---------------- Posterior pieces for this analyte ----------------
  alpha_j      <- post$alpha[draw_idx, j_idx]
  beta_stirj   <- post$beta_stir[draw_idx, j_idx]
  beta_cinj    <- post$beta_cin[draw_idx, j_idx]
  beta_dupj    <- post$beta_dup[draw_idx, j_idx]
  beta_resC_j  <- post$beta_res_C[draw_idx, j_idx]

  beta_vol     <- post$beta_vol[draw_idx]

  # v1p8: analyte-specific irrigation effect
  beta_irrj    <- post$beta_irr[draw_idx, j_idx]

  a_V          <- post$a_V[draw_idx]
  b_V          <- post$b_V[draw_idx]
  beta_res_V   <- post$beta_res_V[draw_idx]

  # sigmas
  sigma_V      <- post$sigma_V[draw_idx]
  sigma_VOLobs <- post$sigma_VOL_obs[draw_idx]
  sigma_Cproc  <- post$sigma_analyte[draw_idx]
  sigma_Cobs_j <- post$sigma_C_obs[draw_idx, j_idx]

  # GP on year
  if (is.null(year)) {
    F_sub  <- post$F_year[draw_idx, , j_idx, drop = FALSE]  # draws × Y × 1
    k_star <- apply(F_sub, 1, mean)
    year_label <- "avg_all_years"
  } else {
    Y_idx <- match(year, years_used)
    if (is.na(Y_idx)) stop("requested year not in years_used")
    k_star <- post$F_year[draw_idx, Y_idx, j_idx]
    year_label <- as.character(year)
  }

  # ---------------- Loop over STIR values ----------------
  n_x <- length(STIR_z_seq)

  # link-style (mean function) summaries
  load_mean <- numeric(n_x)
  load_low  <- numeric(n_x)
  load_high <- numeric(n_x)

  # posterior predictive summaries (latent + obs noise)
  load_pp_mean <- numeric(n_x)
  load_pp_low  <- numeric(n_x)
  load_pp_high <- numeric(n_x)

  for (k in seq_len(n_x)) {
    stz <- STIR_z_seq[k]
    RES_star <- rep(res_prop, n_s)

    # mean functions on z-scale
    muV_z <- a_V +
      b_V * stz +
      beta_res_V * RES_star +
      gamma_CrV_star

    muC_z <- alpha_j +
```
